claimText: The Environment Protection (Management of Contaminated Sites) Rules, 2025 explicitly exclude contamination from radioactive waste, mining, oil spills at sea, and solid waste dumps.
evidenceExcerpt: Explicitly excludes contamination from radioactive waste, mining, oil spills at sea, and solid waste dumps.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the scope and exclusions of the 2025 Rules.
distractorHooks: Stating that the rules cover radioactive waste or mining contamination
Stating that solid waste dumps are managed under these specific rules

claimText: Under the Management of Contaminated Sites Rules, 2025, if contaminated land is sold, the new owner assumes liability for the pollution.
evidenceExcerpt: if the contaminated land is sold, the new owner assumes liability.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the transfer of environmental liability upon property sale.
distractorHooks: Stating that the original polluter retains sole liability even after the land is sold
Stating that the State Government assumes liability upon the sale of contaminated land

claimText: State Pollution Control Boards (SPCBs) are the designated authorities responsible for identifying the polluter under the Management of Contaminated Sites Rules, 2025.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question matching regulatory functions to the correct environmental authority.
distractorHooks: Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) identifies the polluter
Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) directly identifies the polluter

claimText: Initial assessment costs for contaminated sites may be covered by the Central Government from the Environmental Relief Fund established under the Public Liability Insurance Act, 1991.
evidenceExcerpt: Initial assessment costs may be covered by Central Government from Environmental Relief Fund under Public Liability Insurance Act, 1991.
examUse: Statement-based question linking the Environmental Relief Fund to its parent legislation.
distractorHooks: Linking the Environmental Relief Fund to the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986
Stating that the polluter must mandatorily bear the initial assessment costs without any government fund provision
